title: Getting started with pairedcountbrms
text: binegbin() builds bivariate count pair trivariate reduction. Three independent Negative-Binomial counts drawn, two observed counts share one : Nshared∼NB2(μ,ϕs)N10∼NB2(λem,ϕx)N01∼NB2(λlb,ϕx)yem=Nshared+N10ylb=Nshared+N01 \begin{aligned} N_{\text{shared}} &\sim \mathrm{NB2}(\mu,\ \phi_s) \\ N_{10} &\sim \mathrm{NB2}(\lambda_{\text{em}},\ \phi_x) \qquad N_{01} \sim \mathrm{NB2}(\lambda_{\text{lb}},\ \phi_x) \\[4pt] y_{\text{em}} &= N_{\text{shared}} + N_{10} \qquad y_{\text{lb}} = N_{\text{shared}} + N_{01} \end{aligned} shared component sources saw; two excesses saw alone. NsharedN_{\text{shared}} never observed — marginalised analytically likelihood — induces correlation pair. NB2(m, phi) Stan’s neg_binomial_2 R’s dnbinom(size = phi, mu = m): mean m, variance m + m^2/phi. Larger phi means less overdispersion. Five dpars, log link: mu brms’s mandatory first-dpar name. bound shared component’s rate — mean either response, mean difference. E[y_em] = mu + lambdaem.

code: library(brms) library(pairedcountbrms)  # bipois(): joint bivariate Poisson (non-overdispersed margins) fit_bp <- brm(   bf(y_em | vint(y_lb) ~ 1,      mu ~ 1 + (1 | vessel), lambdaem ~ 1, lambdalb ~ 1),   data = dat, family = bipois(), stanvars = bipois_stanvars(), chains = 4 )  # binegbin(): joint bivariate negative-binomial (overdispersed margins) fit_nb <- brm(   bf(y_em | vint(y_lb) ~ 1,      mu ~ 1 + (1 | vessel),      nlf(lambdaem ~ lamx), nlf(lambdalb ~ lamx), lamx ~ 1,      shapes ~ 1, shapex ~ 1, nl = TRUE),   data = dat, family = binegbin(), stanvars = binegbin_stanvars(), chains = 4 )  # binegbin_joint(): same model, but y_em is unobserved where em_obs == 0 fit_cj <- brm(   bf(y_em | vint(y_lb, em_obs) ~ 1,      mu ~ 1 + (1 | vessel) + (1 | vessel:trip_id),      nlf(lambdaem ~ lamx + methd),      nlf(lambdalb ~ lamx - methd),      lamx ~ 1, methd ~ 1, shapes ~ 1, shapex ~ 1, nl = TRUE),   data = dat, family = binegbin_joint(), stanvars = binegbin_joint_stanvars(),   chains = 4 )

what: Joint EM/logbook bivariate-Negative-Binomial custom family for brms — binegbin
title: Joint EM/logbook bivariate-Negative-Binomial custom family for brms — binegbin
text: Overdispersed sibling [bipois()]. Returns brms custom family joint distribution matched count pair `(y_em, y_lb)` via trivariate reduction Negative-Binomial (rather Poisson) latent components: `y_em = N_shared + N10`, `y_lb = N_shared + N01`, `N_shared ~ NB2(mu, shapes)`, `N10 ~ NB2(lambdaem, shapex)`, `N01 ~ NB2(lambdalb, shapex)` mutually independent given rates. `NB2(m, phi)` mean `m` variance `m + m^2/phi` (Stan `neg_binomial_2`; R `dnbinom(size = phi, mu = m)`). Five dpars: three rates (`mu` = shared rate, `lambdaem`/`lambdalb` = EM-/LB-rates) plus two dispersions – `shapes` shared component `shapex` shared across two excess components. five use `link = "log"`. Supply excess rates non-linear formula without explicit `exp()` (log link applies ): `nlf(lambdaem ~ lamx)` gives `lambdaem = exp(lamx)`. See `binegbin.R` file header `tnc001-belize-em` project docs (05-07 generative rationale; OLRE-failure / NegBin-resolution finding) NegBin components used instead observation-level random effect [bipois()]. Use brm() call :   brm(     bf(y_em | vint(y_lb) ~ 1,        mu ~ 1 + (1 | vessel),        nlf(lambdaem ~ lamx), nlf(lambdalb ~ lamx), lamx ~ 1,        shapes ~ 1, shapex ~ 1, nl = TRUE),     family   = binegbin(),     stanvars = binegbin_stanvars(),     data     = dat   )

what: Censoring-aware joint EM/logbook bivariate-Negative-Binomial family for brms — binegbin_joint
title: Censoring-aware joint EM/logbook bivariate-Negative-Binomial family for brms — binegbin_joint
text: Censoring-aware extension [binegbin()]. Models trivariate- reduction bivariate Negative-Binomial pair `(y_em, y_lb)` – `y_em = N_shared + N10`, `y_lb = N_shared + N01`, `N_shared ~ NB2(mu, shapes)`, `N10 ~ NB2(lambdaem, shapex)`, `N01 ~ NB2(lambdalb, shapex)` mutually independent given rates – allows EM margin (`y_em`) UNOBSERVED rows. row carries two supplementary integers via `vint()`: `y_lb` (always-observed logbook count) `em_obs` (0/1 flag marking whether `y_em` observed row). `em_obs == 1` (matched) rows likelihood full joint [binegbin()] lpmf `(y_em, y_lb)`. `em_obs == 0` (LB-) rows `y_em`-integrated marginal joint, `P(y_lb) = sum_k NB2(k | mu, shapes) NB2(y_lb - k | lambdalb, shapex)` – separate single-dispersion `neg_binomial_2` `y_lb`, different model inconsistent matched decomposition. lets one `brm()` call pool matched LB-rows one coherent likelihood: `lambdaem` EM/LB bias identified matched rows, LB-rows sharpen `mu`, `shapes`, `lambdalb`, shared vessel/trip random-effect structure. Five dpars, identical [binegbin()]: three rates (`mu` = shared rate, `lambdaem`/`lambdalb` = EM-/LB-rates) plus two dispersions – `shapes` shared component `shapex` shared across two excess components. five use `link = "log"` (see [binegbin()]). share excess level across two rates split directional bias, supply non-linear formulas *without* explicit `exp()` – log link applies , `nlf(lambdaem ~ lamx + methd)` gives `lambdaem = exp(lamx + methd)`. Use brm() call :   brm(     bf(y_em | vint(y_lb, em_obs) ~ 1,        mu ~ 1 + (1 | vessel) + (1 | vessel:trip_id),        nlf(lambdaem ~ lamx + methd),        nlf(lambdalb ~ lamx - methd),        lamx ~ 1, methd ~ 1,        shapes ~ 1, shapex ~ 1, nl = TRUE),     family   = binegbin_joint(),     stanvars = binegbin_joint_stanvars(),     data     = dat   )

See [bipois()] full explanation. **Relationship [binegbin()].** `em_obs == 1` rows family's lpmf equals [binegbin()] lpmf exactly (marginalisation sum). `em_obs == 0` branch `y_em`-integrated marginal bivariate model. package tests pin identities (marginal identity; binegbin equivalence).

title: Joint EM/logbook bivariate-Poisson custom family for brms — bipois
text: **Naming note.** forced naming `skellam1()`/`dlaplace1()`/ `dnorm1()`: `brms::custom_family()` requires dpar literally named `"mu"` (`stop2("families must 'mu' parameter.")`, unconditional). bound `lambda_shared`, rate component shared `y_em` `y_lb` – mean either response individually. `lambdaem` (EM-rate) `lambdalb` (LB-rate) two dpars, plainly named (forced reinterpretation needed two). **`y_lb` travels via `vint()`, second response.** brms's `custom_family()` API supports exactly one declared response column (`Y`) plus optional supplementary integer/real data (`vint()`/`vreal()` addition terms) – mechanism used , e.g., binomial trial counts brms custom-families vignette. *undeclared-response* concept genuinely joint two-count likelihood; `vint(y_lb)` correct fit gap, workaround. mean `y_lb` ** treated brms-modelled response data (missing-value handling, resp_*() addition terms apply ) – fixed, observed per-row data, consistent fact every row used comes matched (-observed) subset. **Order dpars matters generated Stan call.** brms generates `target += bipois_lpmf(Y[n] | mu[n], lambdaem[n], lambdalb[n], vint1[n])` – dpars order declared , vint/vreal args order declared `vars`. `bipois_stan_funs` (stanfunctions via `bipois_stanvars()`) declares `bipois_lpmf` exactly argument order; changing order without changing Stan signature (vice versa) silently swaps rate governs count.

what: Discrete-Laplace custom family for brms (location 0, free scale) — dlaplace1
title: Discrete-Laplace custom family for brms (location 0, free scale) — dlaplace1
text: Returns brms custom family discrete Laplace distribution, location fixed 0, discretised continuous Laplace(0, b) via CDF differencing: `P(Z=z) = F(z+0.5) - F(z-0.5)`. One parameter, sigma (link = "log"), SD; mean always zero. Unlike skellam1/ skellam2, PMF CCDF closed-form (`double_exponential_lcdf`-based – Stan's name Laplace distribution – Bessel function, large-argument branch iteration cap needed). Use brm() call :   brm(y ~ ..., family = dlaplace1(), stanvars = dlaplace1_stanvars(), data = ...)

title: Discrete-Laplace custom family for brms (location 0, free scale) — dlaplace1
text: **Naming note.** forced naming `skellam1()`: `brms::custom_family()` requires dpar literally named `"mu"`; represents sigma (SD), mean. See `?skellam1` Details full rationale. **sigma--b conversion.** Stan's `double_exponential_lcdf` expects continuous Laplace's scale parameter, `b`. Var(Laplace(0,b)) = `2*b^2`, SD = `b*sqrt(2)`; treating sigma exactly SD (discretisation perturbs true discrete variance slightly, keeps sigma scale three families) gives `b = sigma / sqrt(2)`, computed first `dlaplace1_lpmf` `dlaplace1_lccdf`. **Validation note.** `extraDistr::ddlaplace()` implements different discrete Laplace — `scale` argument actually decay probability `p` exact closed form `P(z) = (1-p)/(1+p) * p^|z|`, continuous-Laplace `b` — confirmed numerically match family's CDF-differenced PMF (e.g. `b=3`, `p=exp(-1/3)`: `P(0) = 0.1535` vs `0.1651` ). package's tests validate hand-derived CDF-difference R reference instead (documented fallback package reference applicable), matching CDF-differencing already used `05-04-candidate-family-validation.qmd`'s exploratory plots.

title: Asymmetric Skellam custom family for brms — skellam2
text: **Naming note.** `brms::custom_family()` disallows underscores `dpars` (`stop2("Dots underscores allowed 'dpars'.")`), second parameter spelled `sigmaexcess`, `sigma_excess` package's design notes Stan code comments — two names refer quantity. **Constraint algebra — corrected original design.** natural-seeming construction `sigma = sqrt(mu^2 + sigmaexcess^2)` (Pythagorean mu sigmaexcess) guarantees `sigma >= |mu|`. condition Skellam validity actually needs. `theta1 = (sigma^2 + mu) / 2` `theta2 = (sigma^2 - mu) / 2` (`theta1 + theta2 = sigma^2` `theta1 - theta2 = mu`), `theta1, theta2 >= 0` requires `sigma^2 >= |mu|` — .e. Var >= |mean|, genuine Skellam constraint (sum two nonnegative Poisson rates always >= difference's absolute value). `sigma >= |mu|` `sigma^2 >= |mu|` coincide `|mu| >= 1`; `|mu| < 1` diverge, `sigma = sqrt(mu^2 + sigmaexcess^2)` can produce *negative* theta1 theta2 — confirmed numerically, e.g. `mu = 0.5, sigmaexcess = 0` gives `sigma = 0.5`, `theta2 = -0.125`. package instead uses:   sigma^2 = |mu| + sigmaexcess^2 guarantees `sigma^2 >= |mu|` directly (right-hand side `|mu|` plus nonnegative term), every `mu` every `sigmaexcess >= 0`, equality (minimal-spread boundary) `sigmaexcess = 0`. `theta1` `theta2` sums nonnegative terms (verify: `mu >= 0`, `theta1 = mu + sigmaexcess^2/2 >= 0` `theta2 = sigmaexcess^2/2 >= 0`; `mu < 0`, roles swap) — strictly positive whenever `sigmaexcess > 0`, log link guarantees finite linear predictor. `mu = 0` reduces exactly skellam1's symmetric family (`sigma = sigmaexcess`, `theta1 = theta2 = sigmaexcess^2 / 2`). **Generated-quantities note.** family ** expose `mu`, `sigma`, `sigma^2`, `theta1`, `theta2` via Stan `generated quantities` block. Confirmed via `make_stancode()`: brms declares custom family's per-observation dpar vectors (`mu`, `sigmaexcess` ) local variables inside generated model's `model` block, `transformed parameters` — Stan-scope `generated quantities`, regardless `loop = TRUE/FALSE`. Reconstructing brms's internal linear-predictor variable names (`Xc`, `b`, `Intercept`, ...) work simple fixed-effects-formulas, breaking silently anything random effects splines. `skellam2_dpars()` () reports five quantities R instead, via `brms::get_dpar()` — works formula.

title: pairedcountbrms 0.6.0
text: Renamed: skellambrms now pairedcountbrms. old name named one family; package’s subject comparison two paired count sources, two complementary routes — difference families (skellam1/skellam2, dnorm1/dnorm2, dlaplace1/dlaplace2) joint bivariate families (bipois, binegbin, binegbin_joint). Skellam one member set, longer used one. family names change, fitted model needs refitting: binegbin, binegbin_joint, bipois, skellam1/skellam2, dnorm1/dnorm2 dlaplace1/dlaplace2 keep names, brms continues resolve fit’s log_lik_* / posterior_predict_* / posterior_epred_* methods attached search path exactly . change user needs make library(skellambrms) → library(pairedcountbrms) (skellambrms:: prefix). GitHub repository moves anhsmith/pairedcountbrms. GitHub serves permanent redirect old path web git, existing clones pak::pak("anhsmith/skellambrms") calls keep working. New coordinate helpers, binegbin_mfd_to_dpars() binegbin_dpars_to_mfd(), converting rate dpars joint families take (mu, lambdaem, lambdalb, plus shapes/shapex) interpretable (M, f, delta) coordinates — overall level, congruence, method bias — along SD-scale dispersions kappas/kappax. Pure transforms; fit nothing, fitting coordinates still goes nlf() (documented binegbin_mfd_to_dpars()). inverse reports delta NA f = 1, excess biased bias genuinely unidentified, rather silently returning 0. New vignette, Getting started pairedcountbrms: simulates paired counts known binegbin parameters, fits brm() plus binegbin_stanvars(), checks five dpars recover truth, exercises posterior_predict() log_lik().
